subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Medication Overdose Risks
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Medication Overdose Risks.

hook.text: Acetaminophen is in your medicine cabinet right now. It's also the leading cause of liver failure.

buildup.text: Over-the-counter pain relievers seem safe, but many contain overlapping ingredients.

discovery.text: Taking cold medicine AND pain reliever can accidentally double your acetaminophen dose.

twist.text: Liver damage from acetaminophen can be irreversible—and symptoms don't appear for 24-72 hours.

climax.text: Reading active ingredients on every medication prevents accidental double-dosing.

punchline.text: Same ingredient, different boxes. Check every label, every time.

quiz.question: Why is accidental acetaminophen overdose so common?
options: Many different products contain the same ingredient
It's only available in high doses
